text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Eggs increase cardiovascular risk.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Eggs increase cardiovascular risk.","entailment":"The source explicitly states that eggs increase cardiovascular risk, directly supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"eggs","comparator":"","outcome":"cardiovascular risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Consuming more than one egg per day was associated with increased cardiovascular risk specifically among participants with type 2 diabetes.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Consuming more than one egg per day was associated with increased cardiovascular risk specifically among participants with type 2 diabetes.","entailment":"The source reports an association between consuming >1 egg daily and higher cardiovascular risk in people with type 2 diabetes, which underlies the claim.","population":"participants with type 2 diabetes","intervention":"consuming more than one egg per day","comparator":"","outcome":"increased cardiovascular risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"more than one egg per day","modality":"associational"}]}
